clear.explanation: Your skin appears clear with no significant acne detected.
clear.educational_note: Maintaining clear skin involves consistent skincare routines. Focus on gentle cleansing, sun protection, and hydration to keep your skin healthy.

recommendations.reason: Helps maintain skin barrier integrity and provides antioxidant protection for clear skin maintenance.
Provides lightweight hydration without clogging pores, keeping skin plump and healthy.
Daily sun protection prevents premature aging and helps maintain even skin tone.

mild.explanation: Mild acne has been detected. This typically presents as occasional small blemishes or comedones (blackheads/whiteheads).
mild.educational_note: Mild acne is very common and often responds well to consistent over-the-counter skincare routines. Gentle, non-comedogenic products are recommended. Avoid harsh scrubbing which can irritate the skin further.

recommendations.reason: A beta-hydroxy acid that penetrates into pores to dissolve excess oil and dead skin cells, helping to clear minor breakouts.
Helps regulate sebum production, minimize the appearance of pores, and reduce mild inflammation.
A natural antimicrobial that can help reduce minor blemishes when applied topically in low concentrations.
Maintaining skin hydration is important even with mild acne. Look for lightweight, oil-free formulations.

moderate.explanation: Moderate acne has been detected. This typically includes a mix of inflammatory papules, pustules, and comedones across broader areas of the face.
moderate.educational_note: Moderate acne benefits from a more targeted skincare approach. Consider products with active ingredients that address both bacterial factors and excess oil production. Consistency is key — most active ingredients need 6-8 weeks to show significant improvement. Avoid picking or squeezing blemishes as this can lead to scarring.

recommendations.reason: An effective antimicrobial agent that reduces acne-causing bacteria on the skin surface while helping to unclog pores.
Helps unclog pores and reduce excess oil production, targeting the root causes of comedonal acne.
Addresses both bacterial factors and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, helping with active breakouts and residual marks.
Zinc has anti-inflammatory and oil-regulating properties that can help calm moderate breakouts.
A soothing ingredient that supports skin healing and reduces irritation from active ingredients.

severe.explanation: Severe acne has been detected. This may include widespread inflammatory lesions, deep papules, and extensive breakout coverage.
severe.educational_note: Severe acne typically benefits from professional dermatological guidance in addition to topical care. The recommendations below are educational skincare insights — for persistent severe acne, consulting a dermatologist is strongly encouraged. They can provide prescription-strength treatments tailored to your specific needs. This analysis is for educational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice.

recommendations.reason: Higher concentration antimicrobial treatment that helps reduce bacterial load on heavily affected areas.
Promotes cell turnover and helps prevent clogged pores. Start with lower concentrations and build tolerance gradually. Use at night with SPF during the day.
Pharmaceutical-grade azelaic acid addresses inflammation, bacterial factors, and hyperpigmentation simultaneously.
Severely affected skin needs gentle cleansing to avoid further barrier damage. Avoid harsh sulfates and physical scrubs.
Helps restore the skin barrier which is often compromised with severe acne and active treatments.
Post-inflammatory skin is highly susceptible to sun damage and pigmentation. Daily sun protection is essential.

minimal.explanation: Your pores appear minimal and well-maintained.
minimal.educational_note: Small, barely visible pores indicate good skin health. Continue with your current routine and focus on prevention to maintain pore size over time.

recommendations.reason: Helps maintain tight pore appearance and regulates oil production to prevent pore enlargement.
Keeps skin hydrated and balanced, preventing overproduction of oil that can stretch pores.

mild.explanation: Mildly visible pores have been detected, primarily in the T-zone area.
mild.educational_note: Slightly visible pores are normal and often genetic. They can be minimized with consistent care focusing on keeping them clean and controlling oil production. Avoid pore strips which can damage surrounding skin.

recommendations.reason: Oil-soluble acid that penetrates into pores to dissolve sebum buildup and reduce their appearance.
Higher concentration helps visibly reduce pore size by regulating sebum production and improving skin elasticity.
Draws out excess oil and impurities from pores, temporarily tightening their appearance.

moderate.explanation: Moderately enlarged pores have been detected across noticeable areas of the face.
moderate.educational_note: Moderately visible pores can be caused by excess sebum, age-related elasticity loss, or sun damage. A combination of exfoliation, oil control, and collagen-supporting ingredients works best. Results typically take 4-8 weeks of consistent use.

recommendations.reason: Increases cell turnover and boosts collagen production, helping to tighten pores from within over time.
Deep-cleans pores by dissolving oil and dead cell buildup that stretches pore walls.
Regulates oil production and improves skin texture, making pores appear smaller.
Regular chemical exfoliation removes surface congestion and promotes smoother skin texture around pores.

severe.explanation: Significantly enlarged pores have been detected. This indicates excess sebum production and possible loss of skin elasticity.
severe.educational_note: Severely enlarged pores may benefit from professional treatments like laser therapy, microneedling, or chemical peels in addition to topical care. The recommendations below focus on the best at-home approach. For persistent concerns, consider consulting a dermatologist for in-office treatment options.

recommendations.reason: Strong cell turnover agent that rebuilds collagen and tightens pore structure over time. Start low and increase gradually.
Essential for keeping enlarged pores clear of congestion that makes them appear even larger.
Resurfaces the skin and improves texture by removing dead skin cells that accumulate around pore edges.
High-concentration niacinamide normalizes oil flow and visibly tightens pore appearance.
Supports collagen and elastin production to restore skin firmness and structural support around pores.

acne.explanation: The skin issue type detected is acne — inflammatory or comedonal lesions.
acne.educational_note: Acne is caused by clogged pores, bacteria, and excess oil. A consistent routine with targeted active ingredients is key to managing breakouts.

recommendations.reason: Targets acne-causing bacteria while being gentler than higher concentrations.
Penetrates pores to dissolve oil and dead skin buildup that triggers breakouts.

blackheads.explanation: Blackheads (open comedones) have been detected as the primary skin concern.
blackheads.educational_note: Blackheads form when pores become clogged with oil and dead skin cells. The dark appearance comes from oxidation, not dirt. Regular chemical exfoliation is the most effective treatment.

recommendations.reason: Oil-soluble acid that penetrates deep into pores to dissolve the sebum plugs causing blackheads.
Speeds up cell turnover to prevent dead skin from accumulating in pores and forming new blackheads.
Niacinamide regulates sebum production while keeping skin hydrated without clogging pores.

dark_spots.explanation: Dark spots (hyperpigmentation) have been detected as the primary skin concern.
dark_spots.educational_note: Dark spots are caused by excess melanin production, often triggered by sun exposure, inflammation, or hormonal changes. They respond well to brightening ingredients and diligent sun protection. Results typically take 8-12 weeks.

recommendations.reason: A potent antioxidant that inhibits melanin production and brightens existing dark spots over time.
A gentle melanin inhibitor that fades dark spots without irritation, suitable for all skin types.
Essential to prevent dark spots from darkening further. UV exposure is the primary trigger for hyperpigmentation.
Reduces melanin transfer to skin cells, gradually evening out skin tone.

pores.explanation: Enlarged or visible pores have been identified as the primary skin concern.
pores.educational_note: Pore size is largely genetic, but excess oil, sun damage, and aging can make them more prominent. Consistent exfoliation and oil control help minimize their appearance.

recommendations.reason: Visibly reduces pore appearance by regulating sebum production and improving skin elasticity.
Keeps pores clear of congestion that makes them appear larger.
Boosts collagen production to improve skin structure around pores, making them less visible over time.

wrinkles.explanation: Wrinkles and fine lines have been detected as the primary skin concern.
wrinkles.educational_note: Wrinkles are a natural part of aging caused by collagen and elastin breakdown. Sun damage accelerates this process. Retinoids, peptides, and sun protection are the gold standard for anti-aging skincare.

recommendations.reason: The gold standard for anti-aging. Stimulates collagen production and accelerates cell turnover to smooth fine lines.
Signal peptides stimulate collagen and elastin production, helping to firm and plump aging skin.
Deeply hydrates skin to plump fine lines from within. Multi-weight formulas penetrate multiple skin layers.
Prevents further UV-induced collagen breakdown, the primary external cause of premature wrinkles.
Antioxidant that protects against free radical damage and supports collagen synthesis for firmer skin.

low_confidence_message: The analysis confidence is below our reliability threshold. The image quality may be insufficient for accurate assessment. Please try again with a clearer, well-lit photo of your face.
